    <h2 {...{
      "id": "structure"
    }}>{`Structure`}</h2>
    <p>{`Controllers can help group related request handling logic into a single class. Controller classes are located under `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`src\\Http\\Controller`}</inlineCode>{` folder within your package directory. Here is the sample package `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`metafox\\blog`}</inlineCode></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-text"
      }}>{`packages/
  metafox/
    blog/
      src/
        Http/
          Controllers/             
            Api/                   // Contains API Gateway classes
              BlogController.php
              CategoryController.php
              v1/                  // Contains Controller classes for certain version
                BlogController.php
                CategoryController.php
              v2/                  // Contains Controller classes for certain version
                BlogController.php
             
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Now, we are going to take a closer look into `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`src/Http/Controllers/Api/BlogController.php`}</inlineCode>{` which is extended from the abstract class `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`GatewayController`}</inlineCode>{`,`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`<?php

namespace MetaFox\\Blog\\Http\\Controllers\\Api;

use MetaFox\\Platform\\Http\\Controllers\\Api\\GatewayController;

/**
 * --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *  API Gateway
 * --------------------------------------------------------------------------.
 *
 * This class solves API versioning problem.
 * DO NOT IMPLEMENT ACTION HERE.
 */

/**
 * Class CategoryController.
 */
class BlogController extends GatewayController
{
    /**
     * @var string[]
     */
    protected $controllers = [
        'v1'   => v1\\BlogController::class,
        'v2'   => v2\\BlogController::class,
    ];

    // DO NOT IMPLEMENT ACTION HERE.
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`BlogController`}</inlineCode>{` class defined its property `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`controllers`}</inlineCode>{` to associate 2 versions with 2 different BlogController classes.`}</p>
    <p><inlineCode parentName="p">{`GatewayController`}</inlineCode>{` support parsing the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ver`}</inlineCode>{` parameter from `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "/backend/routing#api-versioning"
      }}>{`routing`}</a>{` and invoke the respective Controller class of that version`}</p>
    <p>{`For example, with the route `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`/api/v1/blog/18`}</inlineCode>{`, routing resolves to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`MetaFox\\Blog\\Http\\Controllers\\Api\\BlogController::show`}</inlineCode>{` with parameters `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ver=v1`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`id=18`}</inlineCode>{`,
`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`GatewayController`}</inlineCode>{` is dispatched with the same method `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`show`}</inlineCode>{`, then forward the dispatched call to method `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`v1/BlogController::show`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "api-fallback"
    }}>{`API Fallback`}</h2>
    <p>{`If a method isn't defined in the Controller class of certain version, the call will be dispatched to the class method of the previous version.`}</p>
    <p>{`For example with the route `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`/api/v2/blog/18`}</inlineCode>{`, routing resolves `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`MetaFox\\Blog\\Http\\Controllers\\Api\\BlogController::show`}</inlineCode>{` with parameters `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ver=v2`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`id=18`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <p><inlineCode parentName="p">{`GatewayController`}</inlineCode>{` will try to dispatch the call with the same method `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`show`}</inlineCode>{` with `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ver=v2`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`id=18`}</inlineCode>{` in Controller class `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`v2\\BlogController`}</inlineCode>{` as normal. But if there are no `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`show`}</inlineCode>{` methods in class `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`v2\\BlogController`}</inlineCode>{`, the call will be forwarded to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`v1\\BlogController:show`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "api-controller"
    }}>{`Api Controller`}</h2>
    <p>{`Now, we will move to see how the Controller classes of certain API version are defined for main action controllers. `}</p>
    <p>{`Below is the sample Blog Controller of version `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`v1`}</inlineCode>{`. This class is located under `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`src/Http/Controllers/Api/v1/BlogController.php`}</inlineCode></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`<?php

// define namespace
namespace MetaFox\\Blog\\Http\\Controllers\\Api\\v1;

/**
 * Class BlogController.
 * @ignore
 * @codeCoverageIgnore
 */
class BlogController
{
  /**
     * View Blog
     *
     * @param int $id
     *
     * @return BlogDetail
     * @throws AuthenticationException
     * @throws AuthorizationException
     */
    public function show(int $id): BlogDetail
    {
        $blog = $this->repository->viewBlog(user(), $id);

        return new BlogDetail($blog);
    }

    // define other methods.
}
`}</code></pre>

`}</code></pre>
